infoActivity.name: Merging and the present step from the center ❓
infoActivity.comment: I love perceiving the beauty of the world and contributing through who I am. Meaning and strength lie in metaphysical connection. What I am is expressed by the body — and what I am becoming points to the next step.

activities.name: Space
properties: 🧭 How am I working on it?

en.title: Info

quotes.text: I am not what happened to me, I am what I choose to become.
quotes.author: Carl Gustav Jung

quotes.text: No problem can be solved from the same level of consciousness that created it.
quotes.author: Albert Einstein

quotes.text: Where consciousness (attention) wanders, there energy flows.
quotes.author: Gurudeva

en.why:
The world can be described as a system of interconnected structures of various types and levels. Running through all these structures is one common element — the order of things themselves, being as such. In this light, the present moment is not a coincidence, but a given — the best possible version of what is.

The flow of reality and change cannot be stopped — it is the certainty of passing. Our senses convey only a tiny fraction of available information, and so it can be said that the world arises primarily within ourselves. Roughly 95% of all processes take place beyond conscious control, deep in the realm of the unconscious.

What to do with that 5% of conscious capacity — roughly 48 minutes a day — those gentle flashes, the only thing we truly have? They can be found in the space between emotion and mind (heart), between situation and state (truth), between form and content (meaning). It is the place where a person expands the spectrum of reality grasped by consciousness.

The first step is to name one's why. It is the energy of energies, the inner anchor from which everything else flows. Without being rooted in meaning, direction, or conviction, we cannot endure in the long run — only from why can a meaningful how and a concrete what grow. Why is the meaning, essence, and direction we gladly return to — recollection.
en.noteWhy: I love perceiving the beauty of the world and contributing through who I am. The meaning and strength lie in metaphysical connection. What I am is expressed by the body — and what I am becoming points to the next step.
en.body:
The second step can be understood as the effort to distinguish, through attention, the how and the what. The mind is usually occupied with either expression — situation, form, action — or with concrete specifics — state, content, abstraction. Once we see one or the other, reality brightens and can be grasped anew. If we recall our why and consciously take even a small step in that direction, we are capable of more than we realize in that moment — an expansion of the spectrum. Everything can be placed in the context of reality — integrated through an intentional merging with our true nature in why.

ℹ️ A note on the app: Conscious steps can be measured — they are the moments when a person is dealing with some how or what, while being aligned with the full spectrum of reality and with their own why. On the Today page we identify the type of experience, thereby beginning to observe what is being experienced, we recall our why as direction, and place everything in the context of reality. That is the win — but now it is about the next step: a small, gentle step forward.

How many times have we acted in full alignment with the present moment, without even being aware of it? When we enter a state of flow during an activity — that is, a pure experience of skill with a clear goal and immediate feedback — consciousness merges with reality entirely naturally.

But there is the mind, projecting the conscious component into a person's psyche — for example in the form of ego (identification), desire (craving), or will (direction). The mind provides us with forms of cognition: cleverness (practical orientation in the world), reason (analytical thinking), and wisdom (transcending the particular toward the whole). At the same time, it gives us the capacity to create on three planes of reality — the physical (objective, material, measurable), the inner (subjective, immaterial, immeasurable), and the social (intersubjective, constituted by agreement, not by fact).

The potential lies in the mind, which is the transcendental component of consciousness. We can be aware of the very process of awareness, but we can equally identify with what is being made aware of (what, how, why) — and in so doing we ultimately experience the correlate of what we are: a constant process of becoming. Thought → word → deed → habit → character → destiny.

If we do not identify with the contents of consciousness or with external circumstances, we remain ourselves — in the most authentic form of ourselves — in alignment with reality, which always works for us, because it fully merges with our what, how, and why.

featuredQuote.text: Know thyself and you will know the world (you are that world).
featuredQuote.author: Oracle at Delphi
